introTxt: Frontend developer building sharp, resilient interfaces for brands, cultural institutions, and products that need more than a landing page facelift. I work across agency teams and freelance builds, carrying projects from first structure to final deploy without losing taste, clarity, or momentum.

sections.text: Work built inside agency teams, where design ambition, stakeholder reality, and delivery pressure all meet in the same room. I usually sit where interface quality, frontend architecture, and practical shipping decisions overlap.
Projects I lead from scope to launch: product thinking, UI implementation, content structure, integrations, accessibility, deployment, and the occasional controlled chaos.

cards.title: Background
cards.content: Before tech, I spent four years as Venue Manager at Forum Przestrzenie Kraków, a cultural complex with bars, restaurants, concerts, and a constant stream of moving parts. Managing teams, budgets, vendors, and event pressure taught me how to make decisions quickly, communicate clearly, and keep systems working when conditions are less than ideal.

cards.title: Now
cards.content: I moved into web development in 2021 and have since worked across cultural platforms, e-commerce, admin systems, and product interfaces for clients in Berlin and Kraków. In 2024 I began taking on freelance work independently, leading projects from scope through production. I’m especially drawn to complex interfaces, accessibility, legacy modernization, and AI-assisted workflows that are actually useful.

cards.title: Off the Clock
cards.content: Outside work, I still stay close to the world I came from: music, events, late nights, and strange energy. I DJ under the name Eggplant Man, and when I need a full reset I go looking for wind and water through kitesurfing and surfing. Based between Kraków and Warsaw, working in English and Polish.

cards.title: Accessibility
cards.content: Accessibility is not polish applied at the end. It is part of the structure from the first decision onward: semantics, keyboard flow, screen readers, contrast, and interaction patterns that respect actual people.

cards.title: Ownership
cards.content: I like taking responsibility for the full shape of a project, not only the ticket currently assigned to me. That means asking better questions early, spotting delivery risk before it becomes expensive, and caring about what happens after launch.

cards.title: Pragmatism
cards.content: I enjoy good tools, but I trust fit over fashion. The best stack is the one that supports the product, the team, and the timeline without turning the whole build into a performance piece.
